text: Debugging is a crucial skill for any programmer. When your code doesn't work as expected, the first step is identifying the bug. You can start by reproducing the problem and observing what goes wrong. A systematic approach helps avoid confusion: check recent changes, test small sections, and isolate the faulty part. Many debugging tools can assist you, such as breakpoints in an IDE or a debugger that steps through code line by line. Additionally, logging is very useful. By inserting messages into your code, you can track variable values and program flow. This method makes hidden issues easier to spot, saving you time and frustration.
